nativeRange.habitat: Reef and rocky substrate in shallow tropical and subtropical waters; grows in grooves, depressions, and crevices among coral heads in moderately protected reef zones

decorPreferences: open rock surfaces for carpet spreading

avoidWith: urchins known to consume calcareous algae
herbivorous fish that may graze heavily on macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish in smaller tanks)

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Carpeting Halimeda (Halimeda opuntia). Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty entry.

summary: Carpeting Halimeda (Halimeda opuntia) is a calcareous green macroalga that creeps horizontally across rockwork, producing a mat of flat, calcified segments rather than growing upright. Generally unpalatable to most fish, it suits high-light display reef tanks and grows slowly by design.
careNotes:
Carpeting Halimeda (Halimeda opuntia) is a heavily calcified green macroalga in the family Halimedaceae, order Bryopsidales. Unlike most Halimeda species that grow erect from a holdfast, H. opuntia tends to creep horizontally, producing a mat or carpet of calcified flat segments across rockwork — a growth habit that makes it particularly striking as a living aquascape element. Individual segments are flat and kidney- or fan-shaped, up to approximately 8 mm high and 10 mm broad, with a distinct central rib. Colony spread can reach up to about 25 cm in well-established specimens.

This species has one of the widest natural distributions in the genus, occurring across the Indo-Pacific, tropical Atlantic, Caribbean, Mediterranean, and around Florida and the Bahamas. In the wild it inhabits grooves, depressions, and cracks in rocks and between coral heads in moderately protected reef zones, at depths documented to approximately 55 m.

CALCIUM AND ALKALINITY: As a calcareous alga, H. opuntia deposits calcium carbonate (aragonite) into each segment. Stable reef calcium (380–420 ppm) and magnesium (1,250–1,350 ppm) levels are generally advised, alongside alkalinity of 8–12 dKH. Drops in calcium or alkalinity availability can stall growth and cause segment whitening.

LIGHTING: High-intensity lighting is generally advised for this species. Calcareous Halimeda species have been documented to require ample photosynthetically active radiation to support both photosynthesis and active calcification. Medium-to-high PAR (150+ µmol/m²/s at the placement point) is commonly recommended in the hobby.

GROWTH RATE: Growth tends to be slow relative to non-calcareous macroalgae, reflecting the metabolic cost of depositing calcium carbonate. Slow growth is normal and should not be interpreted as decline.

SHIPPING WHITENING: Halimeda commonly arrives appearing white or pale following shipment, as chloroplasts migrate deeper into tissue in darkness. Coloration tends to return within a few hours of light exposure.

GRAZING RESISTANCE: The calcified segments are generally unpalatable to most marine fish and invertebrates, making this species relatively grazing-resistant compared to non-calcareous macroalgae. However, urchins and some larger herbivores have been reported to consume Halimeda in aquaria.

H. opuntia is generally considered somewhat more forgiving than upright Halimeda species in captivity, as its low-spreading, rock-hugging growth form may be more resilient to water-current variation. It is nonetheless classified as intermediate-level care, given the strict calcium and lighting requirements that calcareous species share.

SIBLING SPECIES: See also cactus-halimeda (H. monile, cylindrical-segment upright form) and coin-halimeda (H. discoidea, large rounded coin-shaped segments, upright form).
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is typically achieved by fragmentation — breaking off a healthy segment chain and placing it against rockwork or attaching it to a plug, where it tends to spread horizontally over the surface. Sexual reproduction (alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle typical of green algae) has not been documented in hobby aquaria.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty entry. Product listing for 'Carpeting Halimeda' (Halimeda opuntia). Confirms scientific name, carpeting/creeping growth habit, calcified nature, general unpalatability to most fish, and shipping whitening behavior.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Halimeda opuntia
additional.notes: Taxonomy confirmed: Family Halimedaceae, Order Bryopsidales. Native range: Indo-Pacific, tropical Atlantic, Caribbean, Mediterranean, Florida/Bahamas. Habitat: grooves and crevices among coral heads in moderately protected reef zones. Depth: to approximately 55 m. Adult colony up to ~25 cm. Segment morphology: flat, kidney-/fan-shaped, up to 8 mm high, 10 mm broad, with distinct central rib.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
